opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: [Son reports] He suddenly dropped his coffee cup, the right side of his face sagged, and now he can't get any words out that make sense.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden facial drooping, right-sided weakness, and inability to speak clearly starting about 45 minutes ago.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden, about 45 minutes ago, while holding a coffee cup
symptom_narrative.duration: constant since it started, not improving
symptom_narrative.character: sudden facial droop, weakness, and difficulty speaking
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: severe — son says he can't move his right arm or leg properly and can't get intelligible words out
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right side of the face, right arm, right leg
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: not applicable — sudden onset without a clear trigger
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: dropped his coffee cup suddenly, right side of face sagging, can't speak clearly, right arm and leg very weak, seems frustrated but still conscious and alert
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: son reports nothing like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: cannot walk or use his right arm at all, seated in a chair

past_medical_history.how_managed: was on a blood thinner but stopped it a few months ago

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: son present and providing the history since the patient cannot speak clearly; stopped his anticoagulant a few months ago due to cost

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: not applicable — patient cannot communicate clearly; son is the primary historian and is frightened

communication_style.typical_message_style: son relays all information; patient makes frustrated gestures but cannot form intelligible sentences

access_barriers.transport: son present but this is a time-critical emergency requiring ambulance, not self-transport
